target.description:
Build target for a specific platform/configuration. 
Used to generate release tarballs and platform-specific sub-packages.
target.markdownDescription:
# Build target  
Build target for a specific platform/configuration.  
Used to generate release tarballs and platform-specific sub-packages.

build.markdownDescription:
# Build tool  
- `cargo`, native compilation on a matching runner.  
- `cross`, cross-compilation via the `cross` crate (Linux → { Linux, *BSD } where cross has a maintained image).  
- `cargo-cross-toolchain`, cross-compilation via [`taiki-e/setup-cross-toolchain-action`](https://github.com/taiki-e/setup-cross-toolchain-action) on a Linux runner; uses host `cargo` with a real cross C toolchain. For tier-2 BSD/illumos targets where `std` is prebuilt.  
- `cargo-build-std`, tier-3 cross-compilation: `setup-cross-toolchain-action` + nightly Rust + `-Z build-std`. The release workflow handles the manual build/package/upload because `taiki-e/upload-rust-binary-action` cannot inject `-Z build-std`.  
- `vm`, built inside a target-OS VM (e.g. OpenBSD via [`vmactions/openbsd-vm`](https://github.com/vmactions/openbsd-vm)). Bypasses the matrix-driven upload-assets job; handled by a dedicated workflow job.

experimental.title: Experimental flag
experimental.description:
Used by the release workflow as "continue-on-error". 
Only valid on tier-3 targets.

libc.description:
npm "libc" field, only meaningful on Linux. 
Distinguishes glibc and musl builds so the right sub-package is installed on Alpine etc.
libc.markdownDescription:
# libc variant  
[npm `libc` field](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/configuring-npm/package-json#libc), only meaningful on Linux.  
Distinguishes glibc and musl builds so the right sub-package is installed on Alpine etc.

os.description:
npm "os" field, values from Node's "process.platform". 
npm uses this to select the correct "optionalDependency" at install time.
os.markdownDescription:
# Operating system  
[npm `os` field](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/configuring-npm/package-json#os), values from Node's [`process.platform`](https://nodejs.org/api/process.html#processplatform).  
Used to select the correct [`optionalDependency`](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/configuring-npm/package-json#optionaldependencies) at install time.

pkg.markdownDescription:
# Sub-package name suffix.  
Final package name is `<scope>/<pkg>`.  
- [NPM Scope](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/using-npm/scope)
- [NPM Package name rules](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/using-npm/package-spec#package-name)
- [NPM `package.json#name`](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/configuring-npm/package-json#name)

runner.markdownDescription:
# GitHub Actions runner label  
Hosted/self-hosted runner labels.

rust.description:
Passed to "cargo build --target" / "cross build --target". 
Also used to locate the release tarball: "runner-<tag>-<rust>.tar.gz".
rust.markdownDescription:
# Rust target triple  
Passed to `cargo build --target` / `cross build --target`.  
Also used to locate the release tarball: `runner-<tag>-<rust>.tar.gz`.

tier.description:
1 = first-class (release-blocking), 
2 = best-effort, 
3 = experimental (failures don't block release).
tier.markdownDescription:
# Support tier  
1 = first-class (release-blocking),  
2 = best-effort,  
3 = experimental (failures don't block release).

libc.description: libc is only meaningful on Linux. If declared, os must include linux.

then.description: Linux targets must declare libc to disambiguate glibc and musl builds at install time.
Only tier-3 targets may be marked experimental.

title: runner npm targets matrix
description: Build matrix for the runner-run façade and its per-platform optionalDependencies. Consumed by npm/scripts/build-packages.ts and the release workflow.

$schema.description: Pointer to this schema for editor tooling.

binaries.description:
Binary names extracted from each release tarball and shipped in every platform sub-package's "bin/" directory. 
On Windows, ".exe" is appended automatically.
binaries.markdownDescription:
# Binary names  
Binary names extracted from each release tarball and shipped in every platform sub-package's `bin/` directory.  
On Windows, `.exe` is appended automatically.

facade.description:
npm package name of the façade (the package users install). 
Resolves to the correct platform sub-package via "optionalDependencies".
facade.markdownDescription:
# Facade package name  
npm `package` name of the façade (the package users install). 
Resolves to the correct platform sub-package via `optionalDependencies`.

scope.description:
npm scope under which platform sub-packages are published. 
Each sub-package is published as "<scope>/<pkg>".
scope.markdownDescription:
# npm scope  
Scope under which platform sub-packages are published.  
Each sub-package is published as `<scope>/<pkg>`.

targets.description:
Per-platform build targets. 
One entry per published sub-package.
targets.markdownDescription:
# Build targets  
Per-platform build targets. One entry per published sub-package.
